# {{< i18n wbs_label >}} {}


## Purpose of This Document

This document defines the **Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)** for the project,
decomposing the authorized scope into manageable work packages.

The WBS:

- Is **deliverable-oriented**
- Covers **100% of project scope**
- Is aligned with **ecological value as the primary driver**
- Serves as the foundation for Schedule, Cost, and Resource planning

This artifact is mandatory **before the Project Management Plan (PMP)**.

---

## WBS – Hierarchical Structure (PMBOK Core)

### Level 1 → Project

**1.0 Subsea Benthic Analysis AI Project**

---

### Level 2 → Phases / Major Deliverables

- **1.1 Initiation & Strategy**
- **1.2 Governance & Value Management**
- **1.3 Data & Environmental Preparation**
- **1.4 AI Model Development**
- **1.5 Validation & Ecological Assurance**
- **1.6 Digital Asset Consolidation**
- **1.7 Project Management & Control**

---

### Level 3 → Work Packages

#### 1.1 Initiation & Strategy

- 1.1.1 Project Charter
- 1.1.2 Business Case
- 1.1.3 Strategy & Governance (Value Stream, OKRs)
- 1.1.4 Stakeholder Identification

#### 1.2 Governance & Value Management

- 1.2.1 OKR Definition & Alignment
- 1.2.2 KPI Definition & Baseline
- 1.2.3 Stage‑Gate Framework
- 1.2.4 Governance Reviews

#### 1.3 Data & Environmental Preparation

- 1.3.1 AUV Image Ingestion
- 1.3.2 Metadata & Spatial Data Preparation
- 1.3.3 Image Quality Assessment
- 1.3.4 Environmental Annotation Guidelines

#### 1.4 AI Model Development

- 1.4.1 Annotation & Labeling (HITL)
- 1.4.2 Feature Engineering
- 1.4.3 Model Training (Binary Classification)
- 1.4.4 Model Inference Pipeline

#### 1.5 Validation & Ecological Assurance

- 1.5.1 Model Performance Evaluation
- 1.5.2 Ecological Validation by SMEs
- 1.5.3 Error Analysis & Mitigation
- 1.5.4 MVP Ecological Acceptance

#### 1.6 Digital Asset Consolidation

- 1.6.1 Dataset Versioning
- 1.6.2 Model Artifact Management
- 1.6.3 Traceability & Audit Support
- 1.6.4 Reuse Readiness Assessment

#### 1.7 Project Management & Control

- 1.7.1 Planning & Monitoring
- 1.7.2 Risk & Issue Management
- 1.7.3 Communication & Reporting
- 1.7.4 Closure & Lessons Learned

---

## Visual Representation 1 — WBS Tree (Conceptual)

```
1.0 Subsea Benthic Analysis AI
├── 1.1 Initiation & Strategy
├── 1.2 Governance & Value
├── 1.3 Data & Environment
├── 1.4 AI Development
├── 1.5 Ecological Validation
├── 1.6 Digital Assets
└── 1.7 Project Management
```

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  A["1.0 Subsea Benthic Analysis AI Project"]

  A --> B["1.1 Initiation & Strategy"]
  A --> C["1.2 Governance & Value Management"]
  A --> D["1.3 Data & Environmental Preparation"]
  A --> E["1.4 AI Model Development"]
  A --> F["1.5 Validation & Ecological Assurance"]
  A --> G["1.6 Digital Asset Consolidation"]
  A --> H["1.7 Project Management & Control"]

  B --> B1["Charter & Business Case"]
  B --> B2["Strategy & OKRs"]
  B --> B3["Stakeholder Register"]

  D --> D1["AUV Image Ingestion"]
  D --> D2["Metadata & Spatial Data"]
  D --> D3["Image Quality Assessment"]
  D --> D4["Environmental Annotation Guidelines"]

  E --> E1["Annotation (HITL)"]
  E --> E2["Model Training"]
  E --> E3["Inference Pipeline"]

  F --> F1["Ecological Validation (SME)"]
  F --> F2["Error Analysis"]
  F --> F3["MVP Acceptance"]

  G --> G1["Dataset Versioning"]
  G --> G2["Model Artifacts"]
  G --> G3["Audit & Traceability"]
```


---

## Visual Representation 2 — Value-Oriented WBS (Ecology-First)

```
Ecological Confidence
     │
     ├── Environmental Data Preparation (1.3)
     ├── Ecological Validation (1.5)
     │
     └── AI as Enabler
           ├── Annotation & Model (1.4)
           └── Digital Assets (1.6)
```

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  ECO["Ecological Confidence & Environmental Protection"]

  ECO --> P1["Environmental Data Preparation"]
  ECO --> P2["Ecological Validation & Assurance"]

  P1 --> D1["AUV Image Ingestion"]
  P1 --> D2["Quality & Noise Reduction"]

  P2 --> V1["SME Validation"]
  P2 --> V2["False-Negative Control"]

  ECO --> EN["AI as Strategic Enabler"]

  EN --> A1["AI Model Development"]
  EN --> A2["Automated Screening"]
  EN --> A3["Digital Asset Creation"]

  A3 --> L1["Reusable Environmental Intelligence"]
```

**Interpretation:**  

AI exists to scale ecological understanding, not the reverse.

---

## Visual Representation 3 — WBS × OKR/KR Mapping (Excerpt)

| WBS Element | OKR / KR Supported |
|-----------|--------------------|
| 1.3 Data Preparation | KR3, KR5 |
| 1.4 AI Development | KR1, KR2, KR3 |
| 1.5 Ecological Validation | KR3 (Primary) |
| 1.6 Digital Assets | KR4, KR5 |
| 1.7 Project Control | All KRs |

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  W1["1.3 Data Preparation"]
  W2["1.4 AI Development"]
  W3["1.5 Ecological Validation"]
  W4["1.6 Digital Assets"]

  KR1["KR1<br/>Expert Focus"]
  KR2["KR2<br/>Faster Environmental Insight"]
  KR3["KR3<br/>Ecological Data Protection"]
  KR4["KR4<br/>Cross-Field Consistency"]
  KR5["KR5<br/>Reusable Environmental Asset"]

  W1 --> KR3
  W1 --> KR5

  W2 --> KR1
  W2 --> KR2
  W2 --> KR3

  W3 --> KR3

  W4 --> KR4
  W4 --> KR5
```

---

## WBS Governance Rules

- Every WBS element must support at least one KR
- No work package may proceed without ecological validation where applicable
- Changes to WBS require formal change control

---

## WBS Baseline

This WBS becomes the **scope baseline** upon approval and is the authoritative
reference for:

- Schedule development
- Cost estimation
- Resource planning
